steps.title: Shift each option upward
steps.content: The displayed graph is $y=f(x)+2$, so each possible formula for $f$ should be shifted up by $2$ before comparing it with the graph.

steps.title: Match the shifted graph
steps.content:
The shifted model from $f(x)=-3^x+3$ is:
$$
y=-3^x+5
$$
This has the point placement and horizontal behavior shown in the graph.

steps.title: Choose the original function
steps.content: Since the graph shown is already $2$ units above $f$, the original function is the one whose upward shift matched the graph. The answer is B.
